title: Sarehole / Keel Drive (Tolkien area, day out)

main_route.normalized_name: Sarehole Mill, Hall Green, Birmingham, UK
main_route.original_wording: Rosie's Rd, Keel Drive, next to a field. Near Sarehole Mill.
main_route.excerpt: We drove to Tolkein's area & parked outside Rosie's Rd, Keel Drive, next to a field. A lovely afternoon, the children climbing up sloping tree trunks. Near Sarehole Mill.

boundary_flags: Page B_0276_R is marked mixed_start=true; the April 9th entry shares the page with a later April 22nd entry that belongs to a different outing. Only the April 9th content is included here.
summary: A Palm Sunday day trip on 9 April 2017 to Tolkien's childhood area in south Birmingham — the family parked on Keel Drive near Sarehole Mill and spent a lovely afternoon with Betty, Walter, Olive and au pair Paola, the children climbing sloping tree trunks. Thomas and Naomi were separately climbing Ben Nevis that day.
extraction_notes: This is a same-day outing entirely within Birmingham. The diary names 'Rosie's Rd' and 'Keel Drive' as the parking spot, with Sarehole Mill cited as nearby — all in the Hall Green district of Birmingham, Tolkien's childhood neighbourhood. Ben Nevis is mentioned only as where Thomas & Naomi went and is NOT a stop on this trip. The April 22nd content on the same page (Green Man, Bristol, Cardiff references) belongs to a separate outing and is excluded. Core family members not listed as travellers here since the diary describes this as an outing with friends/extended family (Betty, Walter, Olive, Paola); Simon and Eve are the diary writers and presumed present.
